title: Anticipate the future through scenarios — perspective aphorism
html:
TL;DR

  • June 25, 2019 two-line aphorism: title "Anticipate the future through scenarios", body "Different perspective and angles" — earliest dated scenario-planning note in the vault.
  • One day before "same 24 hours as Bill Gates" — confirms the late-June 2019 cluster of operating-frame aphorisms Yahir was loading in pre-senior-year.
  • "Different perspective and angles" — early articulation of the scenario thinking frame later refined as regret minimization and memento mori filtering.
  • Sits in the aphorism as mantra register — title is the principle, body is the gloss.
  • Two days before the destiny aphorism same week — Yahir was in frame-collecting mode mid-summer.

Key quote

"Anticipate the future through scenarios. Different perspective and angles."

Why it matters in the graph

The note is 17 words but founds a thread: scenario-planning as a literal decision-filter Yahir explicitly adopts at age 17. The frame matures later into:

  • regret minimization framework — Bezos-derived, but anchored in this same imagine-the-counterfactual habit
  • memento mori decision filter — death-as-scenario-end-state
  • scenario thinking as the named operating concept

The mid-2019 aphorism cluster (destiny / 24 hour leverage / this note) represents a week of frame-loading before the busy summer of YEBW, pre-senior-year prep, and the Alexandria meeting. Yahir was building the operating system he'd run on for the next chapter.

Wikilinks

related.label: Goal and time frame — same 24 hours as Bill Gates
